name: gen3users

metadata.name: gen3users
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Utils for Gen3 Commons user management

documentation:
# gen3users

Utils for Gen3 commons user management.

## user.yaml validation

Validate a `user.yaml` file using the CLI:
```
pip install gen3users
gen3users validate first_user.yaml second_user.yaml
```

Validate a `user.yaml` file in a Python script:
```
from gen3users.validation import validate_user_yaml

file_name = "my-gen3-commons/user.yaml"
with open(file_name, "r") as f:
    user_yaml = f.read()
    validate_user_yaml(user_yaml, file_name)
```

## user.yaml conversion

Converts a `user.yaml` file from the old format to the new ABAC-based centralized auth format, required by the latest Fence and Arborist.

Convert a `user.yaml` file using the CLI:
```
pip install gen3users
gen3users convert old_user.yaml [new_user.yaml]
```

Convert a `user.yaml` in a Python script:
```
from gen3users.conversion import convert_old_user_yaml_to_new_user_yaml

src_file_name = "my-gen3-commons/old_user.yaml"
dst_file_name = "my-gen3-commons/new_user.yaml"
with open (src_file_name, "r") as f:
        user_yaml = f.read()
        convert_old_user_yaml_to_new_user_yaml(user_yaml, dst_file_name)

```

functions.name: gen3users.main.main
documentation: Utils for Gen3 commons user management.

functions.name: gen3users.main.validate
documentation: Validate one or more user.yaml FILES.

functions.name: gen3users.main.convert
documentation: Convert a user.yaml FILE to the new format. If a DESTINATION is provided, saves the result as a file. Otherwise, print the result.

functions.name: gen3users.conversion.add_basic_roles
documentation:
Generates basic roles from BASIC_ROLES and add them to the user.yaml
role list.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.

functions.name: gen3users.conversion.auth_id_to_resource_path
documentation:
Recursively searches the resource tree for a program or project
named auth_id.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.
    auth_id (str): Program or project name as defined in user access.

Returns:
    str: resource path for this auth_id if it exist, None otherwise.

functions.name: gen3users.conversion.auth_id_to_resource_path_recursive
documentation:
Recursively searches the resource tree for a program or project
named auth_id.

Args:
    root (str): current resource path.
    resource (dict): current resource.
    auth_id (str): Program or project name as defined in user access.

Returns:
    str: resource path for this auth_id if it exist, None otherwise.

functions.name: gen3users.conversion.convert_old_user_yaml_to_new_user_yaml
documentation:
Converts a user.yaml file to the new format required by the
latest fence and arborist.

Args:
    user_yaml (str): Contents of a user.yaml file.
    dest_path (str, optional): Destination file. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    str: Contents of the new user.yaml file.

modules.name: gen3users.user_security
documentation: Module for checking user yaml configurations for security concerns

functions.name: gen3users.user_security.check_yaml
documentation:
Scans a user.yaml file for occurrences of a given phsid to check if it is open access
Must store a fine-grain github PAT with read access to the commons-user repository
`export USER_YAML_GITHUB_PAT=<your-token>`
Args:
    resource_names (list): A list of resources to check for
    path (str): the relative path from the repository the user.yaml file is hosted on
    repo (str): the repository the user.yaml file is hosted on
    verbose (bool): if true scans entire user.yaml file and finds any instance of specified resources

functions.name: gen3users.user_security._resource_recursion
documentation: recurses through nested subresources in order to find instance of phsid

functions.name: gen3users.validation.assert_and_log
documentation:
If an assertion fails, logs the provided error message.

Args:
    assertion_success (bool): result of an assertion.
    error_message (str): message to display if the assertion failed.

Return:
    assertion_success(bool): result of the assertion.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_user_yaml
documentation:
Runs all the validation checks against a user.yaml file.

Args:
    user_yaml (str): Contents of a user.yaml file.
    name (str): Displayable name of the tested file.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.get_field_from_list
documentation:
Returns the field value for each dictionary in a list of dictionaries.

Args:
    li (list[dict]): List of dictionaries.
    field (str): Name of the property to select.

Return:
    (list)

functions.name: gen3users.validation.resource_tree_to_paths
documentation:
Builds the list of existing resource paths from the authz.resources
section of the user.yaml.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.

Return:
    paths_list(list[str]): list of existing resource paths.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.resource_tree_to_paths_recursive
documentation:
Recursively builds resource paths by appending a slash.

Args:
    root (str): current resource path.
    paths_list (list[str]): list of existing resource paths.
    resource (dict): current resource.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_resource_syntax_recursive
documentation:
Recursively validates the resource tree by checking the syntax
of subresources.

Args:
    resource (dict): current resource.

Return:
    ok(bool): whether the validation succeeded.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_syntax
documentation:
Validates the syntax of the user.yaml by checking expected sections and
fields are present. Also checks that there are no duplicates in key fields.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.

Return:
    ok(bool): whether the validation succeeded.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_groups
documentation:
Validates the "groups" section of the user.yaml by checking that the users
and policies used in the groups are defined in the lists of users and in
the list of policies respectively.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.

Return:
    ok(bool): whether the validation succeeded.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_policies
documentation:
Validates the "policies" section of the user.yaml by checking that the
roles and resources used in the policies are defined in the list of roles
and in the resource tree respectively.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.

Return:
    ok(bool): whether the validation succeeded.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_clients
documentation:
Validates the "clients" section of the user.yaml by checking that the policies assigned to the client are defined in the lists of policies.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.

Return:
    ok(bool): whether the validation succeeded.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_users
documentation:
Validates the "users" section of the user.yaml by checking that the
policies assigned to the users are defined in the list of policies.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.

Return:
    ok(bool): whether the validation succeeded.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_roles
documentation:
Validates the "roles" section of the user.yaml by checking that all
required fields are present.

Args:
    user_yaml_dict (dict): Contents of a user.yaml file.

Return:
    ok(bool): whether the validation succeeded.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.validate_method
documentation:
Validates a method by checking that it's one of the known methods.
Useful to flag common mistakes such as "read_storage" instead of "read-storage".

Args:
    method (str): Method to validate.

Return:
    (ok(bool), err_msg(str)): whether the method is valid and if not, an error message to log.

functions.name: gen3users.validation.check_broad_roles
documentation:
Make sure 'service = *' is not used for anonymous or all_users policies. This is dangerous because it
may allow users to access things in services that were not intended to be accessible. For example:
- Anonymous users have access to public project "projectA". We use a "reader" role with "service = *".
- If a user has "read" access for service "requestor" in a project, the Requestor service allows them
to see access requests for the project.
- Because of "service = *", anonymous users can see who requested access to "projectA", and whether their
access was approved!
